articles.title: Trump’s Latest Tariff Setback Looms Over China Talks
articles.summary: For the president, a major legal defeat could undercut his leverage as he prepares to head to Beijing next week for high-stakes trade negotiations.

articles.title: Iran Says It Seized Oil Tanker Listed by U.S. as Sanctions Violator
articles.summary: A maritime tracking company said the move was likely performative, given the Chinese-owned ship has an “established history within the Iranian trade ecosystem.”

articles.title: Military drills on the edge: U.S. and allies test capabilities near Asia's flash points
articles.summary: From drone boats to long-range missiles, the U.S. and its allies tested new military tactics in the Philippines, sharpening deterrence as tensions with China intensify.

articles.title: Trump Administration Targets 12 Immigrants to Revoke Citizenship
articles.summary: The targeted Americans are accused of fraud or other misdeeds that can qualify them to lose citizenship, but denaturalization has rarely been invoked in the past.

articles.title: Virginia Court Strikes Down Redistricted Voting Map in a Huge Blow to Democrats
articles.summary: The decision is a major victory for Republicans, wiping away a measure approved by voters to allow Democrats to gain as many as four House seats in the midterms.

articles.title: Democratic Gubernatorial Candidate Rob Sand Is Worrying Iowa Republicans
articles.summary: Rob Sand, Iowa’s auditor, is campaigning for governor with a pragmatic message that eschews strict partisanship.

articles.title: ABC Accuses Government of Violating First Amendment
articles.summary: The network’s argument, made to the F.C.C., is the most aggressive posture taken yet by a television network toward the Trump administration.

articles.title: Colombia's rogue hippos could find refuge in India
articles.summary: In Colombia, a plan to cull Pablo Escobar's invasive hippos is challenged by an Indian billionaire's offer to relocate dozens of the animals to India's wildlife reserve instead.

articles.title: Contact tracing could be key in halting the spread of hantavirus. Here's how it works
articles.summary: More than two dozen passengers left the cruise ship before the outbreak was identified. The race is on to connect with them to monitor their health — and the health of those they've interacted with.

articles.title: California Republican Incumbents Race to the Right as Redistricting Forces Standoff
articles.summary: Forced to fight for political survival because of redistricting, Ken Calvert and Young Kim, both Republicans, are accusing each other of being insufficiently MAGA.

articles.title: As federal government pulls back, Colorado charts independent course with vaccines
articles.summary: As state leaders change laws to make vaccines more accessible, a coalition of doctors, public health advocates and everyday Coloradans is trying to start a public conversation about their importance.

articles.title: Court rejects Virginia redistricting in a blow to Democrats' counter to Trump, GOP
articles.summary: Virginia voters approved redistricting that could help Democrats pick up four House seats. Democrats said it was to counter the gains that Trump and the GOP have picked up in Republican-led states.

articles.title: Rooted in nature, 'Silent Friend' will change the way you see the trees
articles.summary: A new art-house drama tells three stories that span the century — and connect to one tree. Silent Friend will open your eyes to the beauty of the natural world.

articles.title: Emirates Doubles Down on U.S. and Israeli Ties Amid Iran War
articles.summary: The United Arab Emirates has borne the brunt of Iranian attacks throughout the war, hardening the government’s resolve to reassess its list of friends and foes.

articles.title: U.S. intercepts Iranian attacks on 3 ships. And, what to know about hantavirus
articles.summary: The U.S. says it intercepted Iranian attacks yesterday targeting three Navy ships. And, what to know about the hantavirus outbreak that started on a cruise ship.

articles.title: The U.S. fires on 2 Iranian tankers trying to evade its blockade in Gulf of Oman
articles.summary: The U.S. military said its forces had fired upon and disabled two more Iranian tankers that were trying to breach an American blockade of Iran's ports, in the latest blows to a shaky ceasefire.

articles.title: Spain readies for evacuations as a hantavirus-hit cruise ship heads for Canary Islands
articles.summary: Spanish authorities are preparing to receive more than 140 passengers and crew members on board a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship headed for the Canary Islands.

articles.title: David Attenborough celebrates his 100th birthday
articles.summary: He was born before the Great Depression, came of age in WWII, and is still making wildlife documentaries. Brits call David Attenborough a national hero, as he celebrates his 100th birthday.

articles.title: Fallout from the Iran war may include a NATO where the U.S. is no longer its leader
articles.summary: President Trump's decision to leave NATO in the dark before launching strikes on Iran has inflamed tensions and is putting new urgency on rethinking the alliance.

articles.title: As war on Ukraine continues, Russia prepares for smaller Victory Day parade
articles.summary: Russia's annual Victory Day parade will be smaller than previous ones as the effects of the war on Ukraine take a toll.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
